document_meta.content_checked_basis: Live Gloucester and GOV.UK public pages
document_meta.note: Hardened v2.2 adds partial executable rule slices and stronger cross-document references. v2.5 adds sanctions and prosecution policy coverage.
document_meta.change_summary: v2.5.3 adds explicit post-summons special payment arrangement pathways, removes named officers in favour of job titles, and normalises hardship confirmation notes.

summary_outcome.content_check_note: Public-facing Gloucester content corrected where live pages were explicit; internal operational detail remains flagged as unconfirmed.

supported_domains: cannot pay vs will not pay branching

quedgeley_town_council.note: Additional precept applies to properties in Quedgeley parish
quedgeley_town_council.status: See separate Quedgeley spending plans document

applied_transformations.note: All top-level sections from the uploaded council tax schema have been allocated to the populated taxonomy, facts, rules or results documents.
Narrative logic remains attached to source extracts in the rules document where formalisation would otherwise invent policy.
Added typed rules for additional disregards and selected property discounts, plus stronger sequencing and readiness metadata.
Added facts, rules, taxonomy and coverage trace for the August 2024 Revenues and Benefits Sanction and Prosecution Policy using the user-provided policy extract.
Converted compassionate recovery from prose principles into explicit decision objects and elevated SPA to preferred pre-enforcement intervention.
Modelled the summons/liability-order arrangement tier separately from the pre-summons self-serve SPA, including upfront payment, income evidence, SFS acceptance and previous-year debt handling.

evidence_requirements.requirement: Medical certificate from doctor confirming severe mental impairment
Include medical certificate and benefit evidence
Evidence of care arrangement and benefits required
Download and email form to revenues@gloucester.gov.uk with student certificate
Must provide evidence exemption criteria met

evidence_to_maintain: Marketing materials and listings (Airbnb, Booking.com, etc)
requirement.annual_declaration: VOA may require annual declaration that criteria still met

notices.message: The service definition remains in review. Publication blockers and unresolved issues remain and are carried through from the source pack.
This results document is a refactor/completeness result, not an individual resident liability calculation.
v2.5.2 makes compassionate recovery and cannot-pay vs will-not-pay branching explicit, but some recovery decisions still require officer judgement and proportionality review.

coverage_trace.note: v2.4 expands the typed rule set for handoff-grade prototyping.

coverage_trace.original_section: Revenues and Benefits Sanction and Prosecution Policy — August 2024
Payment flexibility and debt support additions — April 2026 handoff
GCC Billing, Collection and Enforcement Policy July 2023 v1
Billing Collection and Enforcement Policy 2023 v1 (compassion, cannot pay vs will not pay, SPA role)
Billing, Collection and Enforcement Policy 2023 — Summons / Liability Order payment arrangements

source_files: Revenues and Benefits Sanction and Prosecution Policy — August 2024 (user-provided extract)
design_principles_applied: service definition treated as the governed asset
channels kept separate from business logic
human review blockers carried into populated outputs
information governance retained as part of the service definition

blocking_issues.title: General DPO contact details now confirmed; service-specific IG sign-off still pending
Customer contact standards partly confirmed; collection and processing metrics still need validation
Data retention periods need formal confirmation
Caller identity verification and third-party access process not confirmed
Holiday lets public facts narrowed; detailed operational guidance still needs confirmation
2026/27 charge table not independently verified from current public charge page
blocking_issues.source: Live Gloucester public charge page still shows 2025/26 figures

release_decision.reason: Core public content is largely review-ready, but operational IG detail and independent verification of the 2026/27 public charge page remain incomplete.

open_validation_items: collection rates and service processing times
caller identity verification and call recording arrangements
holiday lets monitoring and transition handling
source_summary: v1.2.0 (2026-03-06): Normalised discounts, disregards and exemptions into flat arrays with canonical record shapes. Extracted valuation band rates into versioned rate_history array with shared valuation_ranges_1991 definition.
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): Cabinet Member, Service Manager, Corporate Director, CTS policy URL, 2026/27 rates
Performance metrics - collection rates and processing times need validation
DPO sign off - required before publication
Data retention periods - need formal confirmation for all processes
Payment system details - GOV.UK Pay implementation status
Appeals process - Valuation Tribunal timescales need confirmation
Holiday lets - council's monitoring approach needs confirmation
Holiday lets - council guidance document URL needed
Holiday lets - Business Rates application processing times need confirmation
Holiday lets - composite property guidance needed
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): Enforcement policy — full escalation stages and post-LO powers sourced from GCC website
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): Exemption classes — full statutory set from SI 1992/558. 13 unoccupied, 8 occupied.
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): National context section added — best practice guidance, Select Committee response Feb 2024, DEA data sharing, national collection benchmark 95.9%.
TODO: Confirm GCC enrolment in Digital Economy Act data sharing (debt-and-fraud-dea@cabinetoffice.gov.uk)

TODO: Confirm whether GCC has a published vulnerability and hardship policy for council tax recovery. National guidance (MHCLG 2021) and Select Committee response (Feb 2024) expect billing authorities to have a formal published policy. If not in place, this is the most significant gap before the schema can be used to generate any public-facing content. Check with Revenues & Benefits service before publication.
TODO: Caller identity verification — confirm verification process, third-party access arrangements, call recording policy and retention. Flagged in channels, data_privacy and fraud sections.
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): Care leavers discount policy — sourced from https://www.gloucester.gov.uk/media/psgjmws5/council-tax-discount-scheme-for-care-leavers.pdf
RESOLVED (2026-03-06): Fraud reporting contact — https://www.gloucester.gov.uk/benefits-and-support/benefits/report-fraud/

CONTENT CHECK (2026-04-03): complaints process corrected to 2 stages plus Ombudsman after stage 2; customer charter timings added; published DPO contact details added; holiday-let detailed switch logic downgraded from fact to interpretation; 2026/27 charge page remains publicly unverified.
supplementary_inputs: User-provided extract from the Revenues and Benefits Sanction and Prosecution Policy — August 2024, authored by Gloucester City Council policy author, used for v2.5 sanctions integration.

member_accountability.role: Deputy Leader and Cabinet Member for Resources

execution_readiness.note: v2.4 is cohesive and strict enough for build-AI handoff on the covered slice, while narrative-only areas remain clearly marked.

runtime_profile.purpose: Primary response contract for MCP and API consumers.

authoring_profile.purpose: Supporting audit, assurance, coverage and source-trace material.

special_payment_arrangement_projection.plain_english_summary: You may be able to catch up your missed council tax payment using a special payment arrangement.
special_payment_arrangement_projection.user_facing_purpose: Catch up a missed council tax payment.
special_payment_arrangement_projection.next_best_action: Use My Gloucester to set up the arrangement online, or contact the council if you need help.

best_practice: Return facts alongside the best resolved answer.
Where special payment arrangements apply, describe them first as the catch-up route for missed payments and surface online plus assisted access channels.
